summary: Stancil argues that creative work has a generative phase that cannot be scheduled the way editorial work can, and meetings are especially destructive to that fragile state. By treating meetings as fixed and creativity as the flexible block, organizations stretch creative tasks into slow, demoralizing slogs. He proposes rebalancing calendars to allow people to decline meetings when in generative flow, even in data roles.
money_quotes: Inspiration will visit you when she chooses.
Meetings are catastrophic when you’re searching for this spark.
By making meetings the fixtures in our days, we have to schedule time to be creative.
Nothing lowers your standards more than trying to wring results from a dry stone.
Surely we can do better than crucifying creativity on the calendar's cross.
key_insight: Treating meetings as immovable and creativity as schedulable turns generative work into a slow, demoralizing grind, so calendars should flex to protect creative flow.
